description: Proof of History enables fast blockchain operations while ensuring security and decentralization. It utilizes a recursive verifiable delay function to hash events and transactions, creating unique hashes that establish a verifiable order of events over time. Each node has a cryptographic clock to synchronize without waiting for others, allowing Solana to achieve high throughput without compromising network security.
topic_summary: This explainer details how Proof of History functions as a cryptographic timestamping mechanism, allowing for efficient event ordering and high throughput in the Solana blockchain, contrasting it with traditional blockchains that rely on slower consensus mechanisms. [t=0:56]
